title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver disabled runtime shell design draft body candidate review

necessityProof.consumer: Node v338 body candidate archive verification
necessityProof.whyV336CannotBeReused: Node v336 verifies the v335 body intake archive, but it intentionally does not answer whether the body candidate is ready to be archived before any body draft.
necessityProof.whyThisIsNotDesignDraftBody: v337 only reviews readiness questions and stop conditions for a future body; it does not draft body paragraphs, implementation steps, provider/client logic, or runtime behavior.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop before any body draft, credential value, raw endpoint URL, provider/client, HTTP/TCP, Java write, mini-kv write/admin command, or automatic upstream start.

bodyCandidateReview.sourceSpan: Node v336 disabled design draft body intake archive verification

reviewQuestions.question: Why review the disabled design draft body candidate now?
reviewQuestions.answer: Node v336 verified the v335 body intake archive, so the next useful step is to decide whether a body candidate may be archived before any body draft is written.

reviewQuestions.question: Is the body intake archive stable enough for candidate review?
reviewQuestions.answer: Yes. v336 verified route, Markdown, digest, smoke, screenshot, explanation, walkthrough, plan index, and historical fallback evidence.

reviewQuestions.question: Is the candidate scope bounded away from implementation?
reviewQuestions.answer: Yes. v337 only reviews candidate readiness and does not write a design body, outline, runtime shell, adapter, provider, or client.

reviewQuestions.question: What must happen before a design body draft?
reviewQuestions.answer: Node v338 must verify the v337 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and fallback evidence before any body draft is written.

reviewQuestions.question: Are runtime side effects still closed?
reviewQuestions.answer: Yes. Credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client instantiation, HTTP/TCP, Java writes, mini-kv write/admin commands, and upstream auto-start all remain false.

stopConditions.condition: The next step asks v337 to draft the actual design body.
The next step requires reading or rendering credential values.
The next step requires parsing or rendering a raw endpoint URL.
The next step requires instantiating a provider, resolver client, or fake client.
The next step requires HTTP/TCP or managed audit network access.
The next step requires Java SQL, deployment, rollback, ledger, or schema writes.
The next step requires mini-kv LOAD, COMPACT, RESTORE, SETNXEX, or write commands.
The next step requires automatically starting Java, mini-kv, or external services.

warnings.message: v337 only reviews whether a disabled design draft body candidate may be archived; it does not write the body.

recommendations.message: Use Node v338 to verify the v337 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and historical fallback before drafting any design body.

nextActions: Archive Node v337 as a disabled design draft body candidate review, not as a design body or runtime implementation.
Let Node v338 verify the v337 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and historical fallback before any design body is drafted.
Do not request Java or mini-kv echo until a later version defines new non-secret handoff fields that need upstream confirmation.
Pause before any body draft, credential value, raw endpoint URL, provider/client, HTTP/TCP, Java write, mini-kv write/admin command, or automatic upstream start.
